name: Paella-Stuffed Snapper
recipeIngredient: Fish (6-8 lb, Red Snapper, Cod or Lake Trout)
recipeInstructions:
----------------------PAELLA STUFFING----------------------
1/2 lb Chorizo Sausage Links (chopped)
1 c Onion (chopped, 1 large)
2 cloves Garlic (finely chopped)
2 Serrano Chiles (seeded and chopped)
2 tbsp Margarine or Butter
2 cups Cooked Rice
1/4 cup Almonds (slivered and toasted)
1/4 cup Fresh Cilantro (snipped)
1/4 cup Tomato Sauce
1/4 tsp Saffron (ground)
6 oz Frozen Medium Shrimp (cooked)
1. Use a 6 to 8 pound Red Snapper, Cod or Lake Trout. Clean and dress the fish.
2. Prepare the Paella Stuffing as described below and set aside.
Cooking Instructions:
1. Heat oven to 350°F.
2. Rub the cavity of the fish with lime juice and fill with the Paella Stuffing. Close opening with skewers and lace with string. Place in a large broiler pan (without the rack) or in a shallow roasting pan.
3. Mix margarine and 2 tbsp Lime Juice.
4. Bake the fish, uncovered, brushing with the margarine mixture occasionally, until fish flakes easily with a fork, about 1 1/2 hours.
5. Serve with Lime Wedges.
Paella Stuffing:
1. Cook sausage, onion, garlic, and chiles in margarine in a 10-inch skillet over medium heat, stirring frequently, until sausage is done, about 10 minutes.
2. Drain mixture.
3. Stir in remaining ingredients and set aside to use in the fish.
